methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $2.0B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $274M - $1.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 53.2x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.rationale: Same sector (Space & Aerospace), same stage (Series B), post-money $274M

data.narrative:
Aetherflux is a Series B space and aerospace company that has raised $60M+. The valuation analysis employs two applicable methods triangulated against a peer set of 3 same-sector, same-stage private comparables (Aalyria at $1.3B, Hubble Network at $400M, and Sift at $274M) alongside 5 public space and aerospace benchmarks (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL).

The Stage-Progression method applies a 1.9x multiplier to a $2.0B total-raised proxy baseline, implying a $3.8B valuation. This reflects typical step-up multiples from Series B to later stages. The Peer-Median method yields $400M, the median valuation across the three identified private comparables, with a range spanning $274M to $1.3B. Public-comp anchoring proved inapplicable due to absence of revenue data, though the sector median P/S stands at 53.2x across 6 public tickers.

Triangulation of the two applicable methods produces a valuation range of $340M (low) to $4.4B (high), with a median estimate of $2.1B. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around whether Aetherflux clusters with early-stage peers like Sift ($274M) or commands a premium closer to Aalyria's $1.3B valuation. Key uncertainties include capital deployment pace, technology risk, and market timing relative to the broader aerospace funding environment.
